title: Aluminum fell to 3,526 USD/T on May 6, 2026, down 1.39% from the previous day
summary: Aluminum prices dropped to 3,526 USD/T on May 6, 2026, marking a 1.39% decline from the prior day. Over the past month, prices have risen 1.78%, and are up 48.15% compared to the same period last year. This reflects ongoing volatility in the commodity market.

title: Comex aluminium premiums beyond August dip on US tariff rollback bets
summary: Prices of aluminium premium contracts on Comex exchange beyond August have fallen due to bets on US tariff rollback. By Pratima Desai in London. This indicates shifting expectations in trade policies affecting aluminum pricing.

title: US to build largest aluminum plant with 500,000-ton annual capacity
summary: The US is set to build its first aluminum smelter since the 1980s in Inola, Oklahoma, by Century Aluminum, with a potential annual production capacity of 500,000 tons, including high-purity aluminum for defense uses. The project received $500 million from the US Department of Energy and involves partnership with Emirates Global Aluminum to reduce import dependence and create jobs. This marks a significant step in reshoring critical mineral production.

title: Canada's MASSIVE Aluminum Discovery Is ...
summary: Canada announces a massive aluminum discovery near Saskatchewan with early estimates of 6.8 billion tons of alumina-bearing material, part of 50 billion tons of critical minerals including scandium and vanadium. The deposit could support mining for over 2,000 years at 50,000 tons per day, potentially enabling 1.88 million tons of annual aluminum production over 25 years.

title: Aluminium Crisis Deepens: War Disrupts Global Supply | Prices Surge
summary: A growing global aluminium shortage is intensifying due to conflicts disrupting key supply routes, logistics, and production in the Middle East, leading to surging prices. This impacts industries like construction, automotive, and packaging, with warnings of higher costs, production delays, and economic pressure if the situation persists.

title: Aluminum industries latest news
summary: Global aluminium production declined 1.6% from 18.6 million tonnes in Q4 2025 to 18.3 million tonnes in Q1 2026. The site provides updates on bauxite, alumina, primary and secondary aluminium, downstream, and end-user news.

title: RAME™: Breakthrough Salt-Free Dross Processing Technology for Primary Smelters
summary: Altek introduces RAME™, a revolutionary salt-free dross processing technology designed for primary aluminum smelters. This innovation represents a significant advancement in sustainable aluminum production methods, addressing environmental concerns in the industry.

title: U.S. Air Force Awards Contract for 38 Advanced Aerospace Heat Treating Furnaces
summary: Phillips Corporation Federal Division and DELTA H® Technologies have been awarded a U.S. Air Force contract to provide 38 advanced aerospace heat treating furnaces. This contract represents technological innovation in aluminum processing for aerospace applications.

title: Midal Cables Launches Third Edition of Sustainability Report
summary: Midal Cables announces the release of its third sustainability report, demonstrating the company's continued commitment to structured sustainability practices in the aluminum industry. This milestone reflects ongoing efforts to integrate environmental responsibility into aluminum operations.

title: New Downstream Aluminum Fabrication Facility Planned in Inola, Oklahoma
summary: A new downstream aluminum fabrication facility is being developed in Inola, Oklahoma, to convert molten primary aluminum into value-added products. This expansion represents innovation in aluminum processing and manufacturing capabilities.

title: Steel Rebar Futures Surge Above CNY 3,200 Per Ton
summary: Steel rose to 3,240 CNY/T, reaching its strongest level since August 2025, supported by robust buying in mainland China as trading resumed after the May Day holiday. The surge is reinforced by a continued drawdown in steel inventories, which have fallen for seven consecutive weeks, pointing to tightening near-term supply conditions in the physical market.

title: HRC Steel Reaches Highest Levels Since January 2024
summary: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel increased to 1,100.00 USD/T, marking its highest price since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, HRC Steel gained 3.79%, demonstrating sustained upward momentum in the market.

title: NMDC Raises Iron Ore Prices for Fourth Consecutive Month
summary: India's state-controlled mining company NMDC increased iron ore prices for the fourth straight month in May, setting the price for 10-40mm Baila 65.5% lump ore at 5,500 rupees/t. NMDC has increased iron ore prices by approximately 20% in 2026, driven by a surge in finished steel prices and tighter supply conditions.

title: Strong North American Buyer Interest Drives Steel Import Orders Higher
summary: Steel traders report strong interest from North American buyers, with import orders ticking higher according to latest survey results. Sheet and plate prices remained on an upward trend as industry sources increasingly attribute market gains to improved demand alongside limited supply.

title: Ramp-Up of New Ternium Lines Moving Quicker Than Expected
source: Association for Iron & Steel Technology - AIST
summary: Latin American steel producer Ternium is ramping up the new cold mill and galvanizing line at its Pesquería facility in Mexico faster than expected. The company anticipates starting the facility’s new steel shop later this year, boosting production capacity.

title: Steel Industry Reps to Deliver Testimony in Section 301 Investigation
source: Association for Iron & Steel Technology - AIST
summary: Representatives of the U.S. steel industry are set to testify on Thursday regarding the detrimental effects of excess industrial capacity and production in 16 economies, including China, the EU, Mexico, and Japan. This testimony highlights concerns over global overcapacity impacting domestic steel production.

title: US crude steel production improved slightly last week, with the capacity utilisation rate climbing to 80.4%
summary: US crude steel production saw a slight improvement last week, with the capacity utilization rate reaching 80.4%, the highest since August 2024. This marks a positive development in domestic steel output amid ongoing industry challenges.

title: In the week ending on May 2, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,856,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.4 percent
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,856,000 net tons for the week ending May 2, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 80.4 percent. Production breakdown by districts: North East (134k tons), Great Lakes (511k), Midwest (311k), Southern (838k), and Western (62k).

title: Copper futures in the US rose to over $6.1 per pound, the highest since reaching a record-close of $6.2 on January 29th
summary: Copper futures in the US rose to over $6.1 per pound, the highest since a record close of $6.2 on January 29th, as energy prices sank and demand improved. Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding, supported by signs of improving demand in China despite Middle East conflicts. Copper rose to 5.93 USD/Lbs on May 1, 2026, up 0.10% from the previous day.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent disruptions at the facility.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Mitsui & Co has secured long-term copper concentrate supply by acquiring 40% offtake rights to Argentina’s Josemaria deposit. Fortescue completed its acquisition of Peru’s Cañariaco project for approximately C$139 million, amid a wave of offtake deals and M&A as majors race to secure supply. This reflects an increasingly constrained market with record-low treatment charges.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper production from top producer Chile faces increasing uncertainty due to Middle East conflict disrupting sulphur shipments to China, prompting Beijing to curb exports of sulphuric acid critical for half of Chile’s refining operations. In Indonesia, the Grasberg mine operates below capacity following a fatal mudslide that triggered a force majeure event. These supply disruptions contribute to market pressures amid rising prices.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group and mining firms in Congo involved in illicit minerals trade, which impacts copper production in the region. This action targets disruptions in the supply chain from conflict areas. The measures aim to curb illegal activities affecting global copper output.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures traded around $19,150 per tonne, easing from a recent over two-year high near $19,600 due to profit-taking after a sharp rally. Prices were supported by mining quota cuts in Indonesia constraining ore supply and a sulfur shortage from Strait of Hormuz disruptions raising processing costs. Nickel rose to 19,877.25 USD/T on May 6, up 1.23% daily, 16.75% monthly, and 27.17% yearly.

title: NI1! Charts and Quotes - Futures
summary: Nickel Futures price is currently 19,028.71 USD/TNE, down 2.32% in the past 24 hours. Related market news includes copper rising as US-China trade tension fears ease and Shanghai metals gaining after China vows to support industrial growth.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: Nickel futures are at 19,232.75, with a previous close of 19,215.50. A recent article warns of a bearish outlook through 2026 due to persistent surplus despite production cuts.

title: Indonesia nickel makers trim battery-feed output as sulphur squeeze bites
summary: Indonesian nickel processors have trimmed output by at least 10% due to a sulphur shortage caused by disruptions from the Iran war. The cuts affect plants processing nickel ore into mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) for EV battery materials, with facilities backed by Chinese firms like Huayou Cobalt, Lygend Resources, and Tsingshan Group impacted. While overall nickel output remains high, MHP supply is tightening with some firms reporting inventories lasting only one to two months.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, has been notified of a drastic production quota cut to 12 million tons for the year, down from 42 million tons in 2025. The Indonesian government is implementing these cuts to boost global prices of its largest export commodity, though the company intends to apply for a revision.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia has committed to flat-to-down ore production targets of 250-260 million tons for 2026, supporting analyst predictions of nickel prices reaching $20,000 per ton. EV demand remains robust with 20% underlying growth, while analysts expect the market to move into the $20,000+ range once Indonesian production discipline is verified.

title: Tenaris Awarded Three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to Engineering Students
summary: Tenaris has awarded three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to engineering students from King Fahd University of Petroleum & Minerals in Saudi Arabia. This initiative reinforces the company's commitment to developing local engineering talent in the Kingdom.

title: Tenaris Publishes 2025 Sustainability Statement
summary: Tenaris has published an executive summary of its 2025 Sustainability Statement included in its annual report. The statement outlines how sustainability is embedded in the company's industrial strategy amid a complex geopolitical and market environment.

title: Tenaris Receives Ninth Consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions Recognition
summary: Tenaris has received its ninth consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions recognition, demonstrating ongoing commitment to sustainable practices in the steel industry.

title: Tenaris Announces $11 Million USD Digitalization and Automation Investment
summary: Tenaris has announced a significant $11 million USD investment in digitalization and automation initiatives to enhance operational efficiency.
